description: Will in Hebrew, magnificently written. Location: unknown. Dated: Sunday, 18 Ḥeshvan 1318 Seleucid = 13 October 1006 CE. In which a rich man, Maṣliaḥ b. al-Ḥasan known as Ibn al-Ḍabāb, gives his second wife and the mother of three of his children, Raḥel, 200 dinars if she does not marry again, but only 50 dinars if she does get married. He also empowers the elder son from his first marriage, Shemarya, to give his younger brother Abū l-Surrī Yefet his share in the estate only when he, the executor--not the courts-- is satisfied that the boy is ready for the responsibility. Shemarya is also to look after his two sisters Jamīl and Qurrat al-ʿAyn. In the bottom preserved line of the document, mentions a man named Abū Maymūn Wahb b. Suqayr, who appears also in T-S 12.239 (PGPID 1636). (Information in part from Mediterranean Society, III, pp. 251, 283, 411, 481)
description_he: מסמך משפטי בעברית מהמאה ה-11. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
